sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Gregory, presented with right ankle pain and swelling after falling on the ice.
He reports that he was being careful but slipped on an icy patch, and his friend helped him up.
He has been keeping the ankle elevated, icing it, and taking ibuprofen, which has provided some relief.
He denies numbness in the foot.
He has a history of playing soccer, but does not recall injuring this ankle before.
His children are also involved in soccer and his oldest son will soon be playing in a local league.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of playing soccer, which may indicate a risk for ankle injuries. [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: The patient denies numbness in the foot. [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: The patient has been taking ibuprofen as needed for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure, pulse rate, oxygen saturation, and temperature were not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient has ecchymosis (bruising) on the skin over the lateral malleolus and associated swelling.
There is tenderness to palpation anterolaterally in the soft tissue, but no laxity on anterior drawer and inversion stress, and no bony tenderness on palpation of the foot.
The neurovascular exam shows capillary refill less than three seconds with strong dorsalis pedis pulse and sensation intact to light touch.
Test Results: The right ankle x-ray showed no fracture.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are consistent with a right ankle sprain.
Problem list: Right ankle sprain; active.
Plan:
I prescribed ibuprofen as needed for pain.
I am ordering an aircast to help stabilize the ankle and crutches for the next one to two days.
The patient will continue to ice the ankle and keep it elevated.
The patient will be followed up in two weeks to assess progress and determine readiness to return to soccer.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen in two weeks to assess progress and determine readiness to return to soccer.
